free_text: Scattering of slow neutrons
Alphas from 100 mg radium on finely powdered
berylium within paraffin behind a lead cone
Thermalized spectrum
Boron trifluoride ionization chamber at two
atmospheres shielded by boron oxide and cadmium
+ +  converted by S.M.   + +
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrected according last EXFOR rules and Dict.
INC-SOURCE was corrected.

Converted from subent 67000012
10-20% include ERR-S
Systematic errors in the determination of the
scattering cross-sections may arise from hydrogen
impurities, mainly in the form of water or occluded
hydrogen, and from strongly absorbing impurities in
the scatterers. The latter source of error was usually
small, since comparatively pure chemicals were used
in most cases.
Statistical error
The cross-sections were calculated by assuming the
scattering cross-section of carbon to be equal to the
total cross section determined previously (Subent 002)
